label: Project
headline: What are you building?
dek: Before we point you at anyone, we need the shape of the thing. Every good build begins with a clear answer to this — be honest.

options.title: A web application
options.desc: Dashboards, platforms, SaaS, internal tools — browser-first, data-driven.

options.title: A marketing website
options.desc: Brochure, landing page, campaign site — where conversion is the goal.

options.title: A bespoke system
options.desc: Something odd, specific, difficult — the thing off-the-shelf software can't do.

options.title: I'm not sure yet
options.desc: The idea exists. The shape does not. You need a partner, not a vendor.

label: Stack
headline: Which stack suits?
dek: There are fashions in software, and then there is what actually works. Pick the philosophy, not the logo.

options.title: Modern & maintainable
options.desc: Boring tech chosen deliberately. Ruby, Rails, Postgres — tools that age well.

options.title: Whatever is trending
options.desc: Next.js this week, something else next week. Resume-driven development.

options.title: The cheapest thing
options.desc: A template. A plugin. Ship it and hope. (We'll gently steer you elsewhere.)

options.title: Whatever is right
options.desc: You want the honest answer, even if it's unglamorous. Good — read on.

label: Values
headline: What do you value?
dek: Manchester is full of studios. Some chase unicorns. Some chase invoices. The ones worth hiring chase the work itself.

options.title: Craft & longevity
options.desc: Software that still runs in ten years. Tests. Docs. The dull virtues.

options.title: Ethics & transparency
options.desc: Honest pricing, open-source leanings, no dark patterns, no tracking cruft.

options.title: Speed at all costs
options.desc: Ship yesterday. Worry later. (Spoiler: later is always more expensive.)

options.title: A proper human
options.desc: Someone who answers the phone. Who tells you when you're wrong.

label: Method
headline: How should they work?
dek: The last question. This is where the pretenders fall away and the grown-ups show themselves.

options.title: Small team, direct contact
options.desc: You speak to the person writing the code. No account managers, no layers.

options.title: Agency with 40 staff
options.desc: Glossy deck, junior devs, senior rates. A reliable way to spend £200k.

options.title: Offshore for cost
options.desc: Low rate, high coordination tax, time-zone tears. Sometimes works. Often doesn't.

options.title: Freelancer on Fiverr
options.desc: Brave. Let us know how that goes.
